task_completion.description: Verify the agent accessed the CAMEO Chemicals web tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser window is open and displaying the CAMEO Chemicals website (cameochemicals.noaa.gov).

task_completion.description: Verify Sulfuric Acid (UN 1830) was added to the CAMEO Chemicals list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Sulfuric Acid' or 'UN 1830' is visible in the 'My Chemicals' list or search results on the CAMEO website.

task_completion.description: Verify Sodium Cyanide (UN 1689) was added to the CAMEO Chemicals list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Sodium Cyanide' or 'UN 1689' is visible in the 'My Chemicals' list alongside Sulfuric Acid on the CAMEO website.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent ran the reactivity prediction between the two chemicals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CAMEO Chemicals reactivity prediction results page or matrix is visible, showing the interaction hazards between Sulfuric Acid and Sodium Cyanide.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent documented the predicted hazards, reaction products, hazard types, severity, and emergency response recommendations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor window is open containing notes about the reaction. It should explicitly mention the hazards (e.g., Hydrogen Cyanide / HCN gas, toxic hazards) and emergency recommendations.

task_completion.description: Verify the report was saved to the correct location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the file name as 'reactivity_report.txt' in its title bar/tab, or a file manager/terminal shows the file exists in the 'Documents' folder.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually used the CAMEO Chemicals website to get the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the actual CAMEO Chemicals web interface with the chemicals added and the prediction table displayed, not just a web search or hallucinated text.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent didn't just read the scenario file and guess the reaction without using the tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: The presence of the CAMEO reactivity matrix/results page in the browser history or active window must be visible at some point in the trajectory.

integrity.description: Ensure the output file is saved exactly as requested, not just left as an unsaved document.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor title bar or a file explorer window confirms the file is saved specifically as 'reactivity_report.txt' in the 'Documents' directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires mixing Sulfuric Acid (UN 1830) and Sodium Cyanide (UN 1689) in the CAMEO Chemicals tool. The expected primary hazard generated from this reaction is Hydrogen Cyanide (HCN) gas, which is extremely toxic. The final report must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/reactivity_report.txt.
